response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with impressive breadth across web, embedded, and high-performance systems, deep open-source involvement, and genuine Linux expertise. However, this is an executive-level leadership role in German-speaking IT consulting, and the resume shows only basic German against a hard C1 requirement, no formal experience leading teams or owning technical project management, and no evidence of the required UML/modeling or Python skills. The language gap alone is disqualifying for a client-facing advisory role.
strengths: Extensive polyglot engineering experience at strong companies (Stripe, Shopify, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby)
Authentic open-source track record (D-language libraries, GISCollective founder) and solid Linux background
Proven mentor and educator through workshops, courses, and conference talks
concerns: German listed as basic; the role requires minimum C1 for client-facing consulting work
No formal team-leadership, tech-lead, or project-management roles despite ~18 years of experience
No demonstrated UML/modeling expertise or Python, both named as desired or required skills

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal leadership and architecture-ownership experience
Strong senior engineering and open-source background
